activities_projects.title: Stoichiometry of Neutralization
activities_projects.objective: Determine the exact molar concentration of an unknown acid via titration
steps: Pipette 10ml of the unknown HCl into a conical flask and add 2 drops of phenolphthalein
Fill the burette with 0.1M NaOH and record the initial reading
Titrate the acid by adding NaOH dropwise until a persistent faint pink color appears
Record the final volume and calculate the molarity using the formula $M_1V_1 = M_2V_2$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the principle of molarity and stoichiometry to quantitative chemical analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of the calculated molarity and volume readings (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigation of Buffer Capacity
activities_projects.objective: Understand how certain solutions resist changes in pH
steps: Measure the initial pH of distilled water and ammonium acetate
Add 2 drops of dilute HCl to both and record the change in pH
Repeat with 2 drops of NaOH in fresh samples
Analyze why the pH of ammonium acetate remains relatively stable compared to water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the role of weak acids and their conjugate bases in maintaining pH balance
activities_projects.evaluation: Data table showing pH shifts and a theoretical explanation of buffer action (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Kinetics of Gas Evolution
activities_projects.objective: Compare the rate of $H_2$ gas production between strong and weak acids
materials: Two gas syringes or inverted measuring cylinders
steps: React a piece of Magnesium with 1.0M HCl and measure the volume of $H_2$ gas every 10 seconds
Repeat the process using 1.0M Ethanoic acid
Plot 'Volume of Gas' vs 'Time' for both reactions on the same graph
Explain the difference in slopes based on ion concentration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the degree of dissociation of acids to the rate of chemical kinetics
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the graph and interpretation of reaction rates (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hydrolysis of Salts and pH Prediction
activities_projects.objective: Predict and verify the pH of various salt solutions
steps: Dissolve 1g of each salt in 10ml of distilled water
Test the pH of each solution
Identify which parent acid and base formed each salt
Explain why $Na_2CO_3$ is basic and $NH_4Cl$ is acidic using hydrolysis equations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates how the strength of parent acids/bases determines the nature of the resulting salt
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of salt types and hydrolysis reactions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Quantitative Water of Crystallization
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the molecular formula of a hydrated salt via thermal analysis
steps: Weigh the empty crucible, then weigh it with the hydrated salt
Heat the crucible strongly with the lid slightly ajar for 15 minutes
Cool and reweigh; repeat heating until the mass becomes constant
Calculate the moles of water lost and determine the integer value of '$x$'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies mass-mole conversions to determine empirical and molecular formulas
activities_projects.evaluation: Mathematical derivation showing the steps to find the value of $x$ (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electrical Conductivity vs. Ion Concentration
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the relationship between dilution and conductivity
steps: Measure the conductivity of a 2.0M acid solution
Successively dilute the solution to 1.0M, 0.5M, and 0.1M
Record the current (Amps) at each concentration
Research why conductivity increases with concentration but molar conductivity increases with dilution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between total conductivity and molar conductivity in electrolytes
activities_projects.evaluation: Graph showing the correlation between concentration and current (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Chemistry of Bleaching Powder
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize and test the properties of Calcium Oxychloride
steps: Study the reaction: $Ca(OH)_2 + Cl_2 ightarrow CaOCl_2 + H_2O$
Add a sample of bleaching powder to water and add a colored cloth strip
Add a few drops of dilute acid to speed up the bleaching
Explain the role of 'available chlorine' in the bleaching process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the oxidative nature of bleaching agents in domestic applications
activities_projects.evaluation: Writing the reaction for the release of chlorine from bleaching powder (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Enthalpy of Neutralization Measurement
activities_projects.objective: Determine the heat released during an acid-base reaction
steps: Measure 50ml of 1.0M HCl and 50ml of 1.0M NaOH and ensure they are at the same temperature
Mix them in the calorimeter and record the maximum temperature reached
Calculate the heat ($q$) using $q = mc\Delta T$
Convert the heat to $\Delta H$ in kJ/mol
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantifies the energy change associated with the formation of water from $H^+$ and $OH^-$ ions
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct thermal calculation and units ($kJ/mol$) (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Synthesis of Plaster of Paris
activities_projects.objective: Control the dehydration of Gypsum to achieve a specific hydrate
steps: Heat Gypsum precisely at 373 K ($100^\circ C$)
Observe the transformation to a white powder
Discuss what happens if the temperature exceeds $100^\circ C$ (formation of 'dead burnt plaster')
Mix the resulting powder with water and observe the setting time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the importance of temperature control in chemical manufacturing
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the chemical difference between Gypsum and POP (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Amphoteric Behavior of Aluminum
activities_projects.objective: Investigate dual reactivity using balanced complex equations
steps: React Aluminum with HCl and write the balanced equation
React Aluminum with NaOH and identify the product (Sodium Aluminate)
Calculate the volume of $H_2$ gas produced per gram of Aluminum in both cases theoretically
Explain why Aluminum is used in kitchenware despite its reactivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of complex metal-base reactions and the concept of protective oxide layers
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced chemical equations for both reactions and theoretical gas yield calculation (10 marks)
